top_reviews.review_body: I've used this power bank over the last few weeks and really like it. I like that it comes with a built-in cable, so it makes charging devices convenient and easy.I charged my phone and steam deck with this power bank and it charges quickly! This power bank is a bit bulky and heavy, but I don't mind since it comes with a large battery capacity and 45W.It's more affordable compared to the other power banks I've seen with this battery capacity. I got this for only $30, so for this price it's really great. The material feels high-quality.

top_reviews.review_headline: Great Power Bank with Minor Trade-Off
top_reviews.review_body: This 20000mAh 45W power bank performs very well and delivers fast, reliable charging. The built-in cable is super convenient and makes it easy to carry without extra cords. It charges devices quickly and holds plenty of power for multiple uses. The only downside is that it's a little bulky, so it's not the most pocket-friendly option. Overall, it's a solid, high-quality portable charger and definitely worth it if you don't mind the extra size.

top_reviews.review_headline: Long battery life and fast charging
top_reviews.review_body: I’ve been using this power bank for about a week and I’m impressed with the battery capacity. It charges my phone multiple times without needing to recharge it. The fast charging works well and the build quality feels solid. The only downside is that it’s a little heavy, but that’s normal for a high-capacity power bank. Overall, it’s a good product for travel and daily use. I would recommend it.

This is an absolute unit of a power bank so far.The weight can be a bit much for some cases, and I see that, but for a battery pack that is belt-clipped for VR gaming, this has been an absolute dream purchase. No rattling, no flimsy feel, and the built in wire has some serious durable lining to it.Lives true to it's charging abilities so far and I will make sure to update the post after a couple hundred hours of use.Picture provided to assist with size comparison. top_reviews.review_headline: Loving this portable charger for the past nine months! I don't leave home without it.
top_reviews.review_body: I have been a proud owner of my Anker portable charger for nine months. It mainly lives in my EDC bag, but has also gotten used in the home when my youngest child is using her phone but not near an electrical outlet and her battery is low. It came in handy for my teen who needed to have a backup charging source with her for computer-based standardized testing. I feel good knowing that if there's a power outage, I have a reliable charger for my Android phone, MacBook, and Bose headphones (when in trickle charge mode for low current devices). And, when we go out of town...this charger ALWAYS tags along.I love the built-in charging cable—that's a gold feature in itself. I have a few other chargers, but this is my primary for that reason. If something were to happen to it, I would purchase it again. It recharges quickly with my 45W charging block using either the built-in cable or a separate USB-C cable. I am considering buying both of my children one for Christmas this year to replace their Anker chargers (that don't have a built-in cable nor provide as many charges).What's not to love? It's compatible with my devices, provides me with multiple charges, it's reliable, and I love the Anker quality.Update (2/12/26): I bought the violet Anker for one of my daughters for Christmas (see photo). I also purchased a case for mine recently (purchased on Amazon and reviewed separately; it offers nice protection, but it's hard to catch in stock).https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0DGFRD1JB?ref=ppx_yo2ov_dt_b_fed_asin_title

Outstanding product! I like the minimalist U.I. - just one button to push to turn the screen on, & a double click to put it in/out of the trickle charge mode (which you'd never need to do if you just use the USB-C port/cord to charge lower power devices like headphones). It charges my phones as fast as any high quality wall charger, & can charge my phone a solid 3 times before it's needing a recharge itself! I like that there's a built in cord, & especially LOVE that the cord can be tucked securely into the side of the power bank! So many other power banks use their built in cords as a "lanyard" which I really just don't think is a wise idea. Not only is it more stress carrying it around by that cable, but I also don't want a idiotic lanyard hanging off the side & getting snagged on things when taking it in/out of cases, drawers, bags, etc... When I came across this model, it's neatly tucked away cable is what sold me! Couldn't ask for a more perfect power bank!This might not be for you if you want slim & light for pocket carrying as it's a bit heavy. But if you aren't planning to carry this in your pants pocket, the weight it not an issue at all.

Great charger/power bank. Used for several brands of phones, portable games, flashlights, etc. Super fast charging my Samsung Galaxy s23 and s24 ultra without issue. Huge capacity. I like having the cord attached. Nothing to have to remember when going out. Just grab n go. It's a tad heavy and bigger than some other 20,000mah power banks so only 4 stars from me. Highly recommend if you don't care about size.

Worked great during a seven hour layover between long flights. I was able to charge all of my devices (iPad, phone, headphones, kindle) quickly and the power bank didn't even need a recharge before my return flight. The built in cable made charging easy.

name: Anker MagGo Power Bank, Ultra-Slim 10,000mAh Magnetic Battery Pack, Qi2 Certified 15W Ultra-Fast MagSafe Compatible Portable Charger, Ergonomic Design, for iPhone17/16/15/14 Series Only（Not for Pixel）
description:
Ultra-Slim and Portable: At just 0.58 × 2.78 × 4.09 inches, this 10,000mAh Anker power bank delivers exceptional portability without compromising on power capacity. It can offer up to 1.8 charges for an iPhone 15 Pro.
Versatile Charging Options: Equipped with a 15W max Qi wireless charging pad and a 30W max USB-C port, you can effortlessly charge your devices at home or on the go.
Premium Design and Durability: Featuring a sleek matte finish, a sturdy metal frame, and aerogel thermal insulation for cool operation, this power bank combines ergonomic and user-friendly experience with long-lasting durability.
What You Get: Anker MagGo Power Bank (10K, Slim), 23.62" / 60 cm USB-C to USB-C cable, welcome guide, 24-month warranty, and our friendly customer service.
Compatibility Note: This power bank is optimized for the iPhone 12/13/14/15 series with 15W magnetic fast charging capabilities. While it can charge Android devices such as Samsung and Google phones, they will not support the magnetic feature or achieve the 15W wireless charging speed, and will charge at a lower efficiency.

top_reviews.review_headline: Slim, strong magnet, easy to carry
top_reviews.review_body: I bought this mainly because I wanted a thin magnetic power bank that I could just snap onto the back of my phone. The magnet is strong and lines up easily, so I don’t have to fiddle with positioning every time.The thing I like most is the size and thickness. For a 10,000mAh battery it’s relatively slim, so it doesn’t feel like a huge brick hanging off the back of the phone. It’s easy to carry in a bag or pocket, and it’s convenient when I’m out for the day. A 10,000mAh pack like this can usually recharge a phone around one to almost two times for my iphone 17.Charging speed is solid. The Qi2 wireless charging goes up to 15W and there’s also a USB-C port for faster wired charging if you need it. But it does get warm sometimes.Wireless charging does drain the power bank faster than using a cable. For me the convenience of snapping it on makes up for that. I mostly use it on long days out or when traveling, and it’s been very reliable so far.

This Anker MagGo Power Bank is fantastic! It’s ultra-slim, portable, and the magnetic attachment makes charging my devices so convenient.The only minor downside is that it does get a little warm during use, but nothing concerning. Overall, it’s reliable, powerful, and perfect for on-the-go charging.

I’ve been using this power bank for a bit now and it’s been really convenient. The size is one of the best parts, it’s super slim and easy to carry around without feeling bulky in a pocket or bag.The magnetic connection works well and snaps right into place. It stays secure while charging, which I was a little worried about at first. Charging speed is solid and it gives my phone a good boost when I need it during the day.Battery life has been reliable so far and it’s nice not having to carry cables all the time.Only small thing is it can get a little warm while charging, but nothing unusual. Overall, really happy with it.

Works great. A quick charge, so probably not great to use all the time as it's probably not great for phone battery life, but very convenient for airport/travel use. No cords to keep track of, and the magnet strongly attaches the battery to your phone.

This power bank charges with USB-C although it is called slim, I have seen other 10,000 mA batteries that are a smaller in form factor. It connects easily to my iPhone, using the magnet connector. It also charges my phone very quickly. I have noticed. The downside, however is that both the battery on the phone get hard very quickly. My solution to this has been to charge the phone to about 60% and then remove the power bank and while the battery still gets warm, it’s not overly so. For the price, it’s a great value.

Absolutely fantastic power bank. The Anker MagGo is incredibly slim and lightweight, yet still packs a full 10,000mAh capacity. It feels premium in hand and attaches securely with strong magnets — no slipping or misalignment.The Qi2 15W charging is impressively fast for a magnetic battery pack. My phone charges quickly and consistently without overheating. It’s perfect for travel, long days out, or just having reliable backup power.What really stands out is the build quality. The finish feels durable and high-end, exactly what you’d expect from Anker. It doesn’t add unnecessary bulk to the phone, and it fits comfortably in a pocket or bag.Overall, this is one of the best magnetic power banks I’ve used. Powerful, sleek, and reliable. Highly recommended.

name: Power Bank, 15000mAh Portable Charger with Built in Cable, 22.5W Fast Charging, 6 Outputs, Slim Travel Essential Battery Pack with LED Display, Compatible with iPhone 17/16/15/14/13, iPad Black
description:
Portable Charger with Built-in Cables for Multiple Devices: This portable charger includes integrated USB A, Type C, IOS, and Micro cables, making it compatible with iPhone 17/16/15/14/13/12, Samsung Galaxy, Google Pixel, iPads, tablets, headphones, smartwatches, and more. Perfect for work, travel, or camping, it eliminates the need for multiple cables and adapters, providing reliable power for a seamless travel experience.
22.5W Fast Charging for Maximum Efficiency: This power bank delivers 22.5W output with advanced fast-charging technology, reducing charging time significantly. Its intelligent voltage regulation adjusts to the optimal output for each device, ensuring fast and safe charging. For fast-charging devices, just a few minutes can provide a noticeable power boost. This feature enhances user experience and helps minimize battery wear from extended charging.
Charge Multiple Devices Simultaneously with Bi-Directional Type-C Port: This power bank features 6 output ports and 3 input ports, allowing you to charge up to 6 devices at once, greatly improving charging efficiency. With its upgraded Type-C port, it supports both fast output and input charging, making it a versatile two-way port compatible with all Type-C devices. Enjoy faster, more convenient charging without the long waits, whether for your gadgets or the battery pack itself.
Compact and Portable for Easy Travel: Designed to be compact and lightweight, this portable power bank measures just 4.56 x 2.67 x 0.86 inches and weighs only 7.09 ounces, roughly the size of a small smartphone. It easily fits into your pocket, handbag, or backpack, making it effortless to carry around. Additionally, it comes with a portable lanyard, allowing you to hang it on your bag or purse, freeing up your hands and ensuring your devices are always powered up while on the go.
Smart LED Display for Power Management: The built-in high-definition LED display provides a real-time percentage of remaining battery life and current charging status, offering a clear and straightforward view. The LED screen lets you quickly check the power bank’s battery level, so you never have to guess when it needs recharging. This helps you avoid running out of power and alleviates battery anxiety.
Safe for Air Travel: This portable charger meets safety certifications and complies with airline standards, making it safe for flights. Built with fire-resistant materials and smart protection against over-current, over-voltage, overheating, and overcharging, it's ideal for business trips, vacations, or commutes. Reliable and safe, it ensures worry-free power throughout your journey.

top_reviews.review_headline: 5-Star Review: HELLEE 15000mAh Portable Charger – The “I Forgot My Cables” Savior
top_reviews.review_body: Look, I’m the guy who once boarded a 5-hour flight with 3% battery, no charger cable, and the panic of a man realizing he left his dignity at home.Enter the HELLEE 15000mAh power bank with built-in cables. This thing is basically a portable charger that went to therapy and decided to stop making my life harder.First off, those built-in cables? Chef’s kiss. Four of them pop out like obedient little tentacles: USB-C, Lightning (for us Apple hostages), Micro-USB (for that one ancient device still kicking), and USB-A. No more rummaging through the bag abyss yelling “WHERE IS THE DAMN CABLE?!” at 2 a.m. in an airport. Just yank ‘em out, plug in, and boom—your phone, tablet, earbuds, and probably your neighbor’s Fitbit are all juicing up simultaneously because this bad boy has 6 outputs total.The 22.5W fast charging is no joke either. My iPhone went from “please help I’m dying” to “okay I’m back, let’s doomscroll” in like 20 minutes. The LED display shows exact percentage too, so no more guessing games like “is it 40% or is the battery icon just flexing?”It’s slim enough to slip into a pocket without looking like you’re smuggling a brick, and the little loop handle means it dangles from your backpack like a proud keychain trophy. I’ve charged my phone twice, my wife’s twice, and still had enough left to flex on my friends who were begging for outlets.Only downside? Now I have zero excuses for not texting back. Thanks, HELLEE, for ruining my “my phone died” alibi forever.Would buy again. Would recommend to enemies so they have no excuses either. 10/10, cables included. 🚀

name: Anker Prime Power Bank, 26,250mAh 3-Port Portable Charger with 300W Max Output, Two-Way Charging, TSA-Approved, App Control, for MacBook, iPhone 17/16 Series, and More (Base Not Included)
description:
300W Total Output Power: Offers 300W max output across two USB-C and one USB-A port, keeping two MacBook Pros running at full speed.
140W Max Fast Charging: Delivers up to 140W high-speed output for fast laptop charging when connected to a 5A cable. Power a MacBook Pro (M4 Pro) to 50% in 27 minutes or an iPhone 16 Pro to 50% in 26 minutes.
Anker's First 250W Dual-Port Input—Recharge to 50% in Just 13 Minutes: With industry-leading 250W dual USB-C input, recharge the power bank to 50% in 13 minutes or 40% in just 10 minutes. Ideal for high-turnaround moments like busy mornings or airport layovers when every minute counts.
Huge Power That's Ready to Fly: Delivers 26,250mAh (99.75Wh) of TSA-approved capacity to charge a MacBook Pro (M4) up to 0.8 times.
Smart Display and App Control: Provides detailed charging information of the power bank and charging base, and allows manual adjustments of output power on the APP.
What You Get: Anker Prime Power Bank (26K, 300W), 3.3 ft USB-C to USB-C charging cable (240W), travel pouch, welcome guide, 24-month warranty, and our friendly customer service.
Note: Data based on internal lab testing. Actual performance may vary depending on specific conditions of use.

top_reviews.review_body: This Anker Prime 26,250mAh power bank is impressive. It charges my phone and laptop incredibly fast, and the 3-port setup makes it easy to power multiple devices at once. The build quality feels premium and solid, and the battery capacity easily lasts through long travel days. A bit on the heavier side, but that’s expected for this much power. Overall, highly reliable and worth it if you need serious portable charging.

top_reviews.review_headline: Amazing technology, portability, power. What an upgrade to a power brick!
top_reviews.review_body: This Anker Prime Power Bank gets rave reviews and I understand why. I've been needing a TRUE "no-wall-plug-available" option for keeping my MacBook Pro M5 Max running beyond its battery, and this Anker Prime Power Bank is incredibly powerful and basically ensures no interruption of productivity. Amazing sustained power. Also--when there is a wall plug--I can plug in two separate power supplies and rapidly recharge the Anker Prime within minutes. I didn't invest in the charging base because this is for portable use, but the build quality, high-speed 24" charging cord and compact size (for the power) makes this a super-convenient option for on-the-go power. Also, it is really well-insulated so there's no issue with heat. I appreciate the LED display and its continual feedback (both charging and/or powering) so it's clear how much power remains or how charging progress is going. Great value for the quality.

top_reviews.review_headline: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Outstanding Power Bank – Truly a Travel Essential
top_reviews.review_body: The Anker Prime Power Bank (26,250mAh, 3-Port) is easily the best power bank I’ve ever owned. The capacity is huge—I can fully charge my iPhone multiple times and still have plenty left for my iPad and laptop. The 3-port setup is incredibly convenient, letting me charge several devices at once without any drop in performance.What really stands out is the fast charging. It delivers power quickly and efficiently, even for power-hungry devices, which makes it perfect for travel, long workdays, or conferences. The build quality feels premium and solid, exactly what you’d expect from Anker. Despite the large capacity, it’s surprisingly compact and well-designed.The digital display is a nice touch, giving an accurate battery percentage so there’s no guesswork. Reliable, powerful, and thoughtfully designed—this power bank is absolutely worth the price. Highly recommended for anyone who needs dependable portable power

Although this device takes awhile to charge, it’s awesome! I enjoy that you can charge to it and from it with dual ports for even faster charging. This will charge my laptop twice! Make sure you get a cord capable of higher wattage if you want to minimize charging times. The display is a really nice feature. This charger is heavy and really durable, and I’ve never had it get hot. I can’t say enough good things about all of my Anker charging devices and accessories!

OK, so note that I am not a tech geek so this is a 'layperson' review.I travel for work and this power bank is perfect! You can charge multiple devices at the same time (multiple = two). The big deal for me is it's ability to charge my laptop! Too many times I have been in a meeting with clients and have had my battery die (and didn't have access to an outlet). It makes me very happy to have this backup for my laptop!It does take a hot minute to charge, but I am OK with it. It charges faster if you use both ports to charge it up.For me, this power bank is worth every penny!!!

This is a great quality charger. I love the dual charging cables — it’s perfect when I need to power up two devices at once, and it handles that without any issues.The charger feels strong and capable, and the handle for carrying it is very durable. It easily supports the weight of the unit, which makes it much more convenient to move around. Overall, a reliable and well-built charger.

Works amazing, charges everything , laptops pc and Mac ,phones, and lasts all day long most days. Slightly heavier than the laptop power bank. You can use 2 cords to dual charge from Anker power station. I like the fact that you can swap out the cord as my younger sons tend to break charging cords. Easy to use with side button. Also pairs to the Anker app with Bluetooth.

top_reviews.review_headline: Great Product but may have an added cost
top_reviews.review_body: Product performance is excellent. Fast charging works as advertised. Easy to operate. However it only came with 1 USB C cord and no charging adapter was included [which was not clearly indicated until I searched the specs again]. So you either get their power adapter or use a different USB-C charging adapter.

name: 20000mAh Power Bank Fast Charging, Portable Charger with 4 Built-in Cables & 6 Outputs, 20W PD USB C Battery Pack Compatible with iPhone 16/15, Galaxy, Android, iPad & More (Travel Essential)
description:
🔋Double the Capacity, JUST 35% HEAVIER: With twice the capacity of standard power banks, this 20,000mAh portable charger with built-in cords powers an iPhone 16 up to 3 times, Galaxy S24 up to 2.9 times, and Google Pixel 9 up to 2.3 times, perfect for weekends away, business trips, or vacations.(Note: This 77Wh power bank complies with airline carry-on regulations for batteries under 100Wh and must be placed in carry-on baggage.)
Built-in 4 Cables: Includes 3 output cables and 1 input cable, letting you charge almost all devices—iPhone, iPad, Galaxy, other USB-C phones, tablets, and Micro devices—without carrying extra cords or adapters.
Charge 6 Devices at Once: This portable chargers for multiple devices features 6 outputs (3 built-in cables + 3 external ports) and 4 inputs (2 built-in cables + 2 external ports), allowing you to charge up to 6 devices simultaneously. Any input lets you conveniently recharge the USB power bank, while total output adjusts automatically for safe and efficient charging.
⚡20W PD Fast Charging & Dual USB-C: Charge devices at up to 20W via USB-C, delivering an iPhone 16 up to 50% in just 45 mins—much faster than standard 5V/2A chargers, while quickly recharging the USB C portable charger itself at up to 18W—perfect for meetings, travel, or emergencies.
Compact, Durable & Powerful: Measuring 6.33 × 2.85 × 0.74 in and weighing 0.73 lb, this 20000mAh portable power bank packs true long-lasting power in a compact design. Its solid weight comes from four built-in cables and the full 20000mAh capacity. Scratch-resistant, fire-retardant shell with fine diagonal texture offers a premium feel and secure grip—perfect for travel or everyday backup.
LED Display & Low Power Mode: This portable charger power bank features a clear LED screen showing the exact remaining battery percentage (0–100%), so you always know the remaining power. Low Power Mode allows safe charging for small devices like earbuds and smartwatches, ensuring even low-current electronics can be powered efficiently.
🛡️Advanced Safety & Multi-Protection: Compliant with UL 2056 and UN38.3 safety standards, this power bank features a 6-layer protection system against overcharge, over-discharge, overvoltage, overcurrent, short-circuit, and over-temperature. It's built to be dependable, delivering worry-free charging from shipment to usage for all your devices anytime, anywhere.
What You Get: 1 × 20,000mAh power bank, 1 × user manual, 1 × travel pouch, and 1 × official exclusive packaging (as shown in main image). Comes with 24-month support, lifetime technical support, and 24/7 friendly customer service with responses within 12 hours.

top_reviews.review_body: I love love loveeee this portable charger. If you're looking for a portable charger that not only charges your phone, it has a USB port so you can charge you watch and also there is a port for typc c chargers as well that could charge. The power for this charger works tremendously for me! The battery doesn't change too fast. I have an iPhone 14 Pro Max and when this charger is at a full battery, it is expected that it will charge your phone to 100% and still have about 54% of juice left over. It is quite heavy since its a literal block but its the size of my pro max, maybe a lil smaller. I think its compact and able to fit most purses. The heat does intensify but nothing that will rock your socks off.I am on my second one and it will come very faster. I remember i needed a charger asap while on vacation and I was able to purchase another and it came fast. My mom just purchased her fourth so she can give them out as gifts.Safe to say we love this charger and you will too!

For the price point, this charger is great. I got it to replace my Anker USB power bank which did not come with cords. I would have a usbc and an iPhone charger plugged into it at all times and when I wasn't using it I would have to wrap the cords around the charger and stuff it in my bag. I like that these cords tuck away and they are very secure when tucked and won't fall out. It also has its own charger USB attached which is nice. I used to have to flip my usbc around to plug the USB end in to charge my anker. The short length of the cords is a thing to get used to. I can't put the charger in my bag and have the cord long enough to use it while the charger is out of sight. This also makes it so that when it is charging and you are using your phone, in most cases you have to hold the charger in your hand in edition to your phone. Not a huge setback as I just place my phone over top of the charger and hold them as basically one unit. Some people complained about the charging speed of both the charger itself and your phone. I've found that it charges itself faster than my anker and charges my phone just as fast as the anker. I just plug the charger in and leave it overnight and by the morning it has full charge. I never timed how long it took to charge. The digital readout of charge left is nice. The anker had 4 dots and you never knew how close the last dot was to dead. I've had it about 2 weeks now so I can't speak on the longevity of it, but for 20 bucks it does it's job better than the price point I feel. I bought a second one for my sister.
Great product I got my whole family one for Christmas. They take a while to charge, but they will charge my phone about three times.

I love this portable charger! It's cute, works as intended, and has all the chargers you'd need. I take it with me on trips, when I'm out in the city all day, or to concerts. I can charge my phone multiple times before the battery life runs out. The battery life hasn't decreased even after months of using it. My coworker used mine and loved it so much she bought one for herself to take on vacation.

top_reviews.review_headline: Good quality charger for the price
top_reviews.review_body: This portable charger is perfect for on the go and usually can charge my phone twice before needing to be charged itself. My one issue is that the attached chargers stopped working.

Please forgive me I did a second review to correct this because I didn't realize that the top part with the handle is the actual charger I think anyway . I do love it no matter what it takes a long time to charge it seems but you know when you first get a battery that that's usually The wave so far so good but I was wrong I'm sorry I think that a little charger that's folded on the box is the actual handle part is you see coming out .... Get one it's great it's got so many chargers you could I mean it's great because everybody's always saying you have a charger and nobody really has this is forever I use it in the car too

top_reviews.review_headline: My first portable charger - would buy again.
top_reviews.review_body: I bought this for weekends we spend at our kid’s university. He’s in the band and we go to all the home games. We spend all day on campus at pep rallies and campus activities. Photos and videos deplete my battery before the day is over and I can plug in. I bought this to carry in my purse so I can make it to the end of the day as I take lots of photos and videos. Charged my iPhone fairly quickly. I’ve never used it to go from almost empty to full, so I’m not sure how quickly a full charge takes. I do short bursts of about 15 minutes and it charges quickly enough to get me through another hour. I plug it in between uses to keep it full. Love the size and the multiple types of charging ports it has. Works for my earbuds (which are not Apple). Slim and compact. Easy to carry in my crossbody purse and my small clear bag for the stadium. Would buy again.

top_reviews.review_headline: Great and convenient! Slow pack charge once dead
top_reviews.review_body: It charges my iPhone 3 times before the pack dies. The chords it comes with is super duper convenient!I will say that it takes about 8 hours to fully charge from dead, so its a pretty slow charging time on the pack itself which is why it is not 5 stars.

name: MARBERO Portable Power Station 88Wh Camping Lithium Battery Solar Generator Fast Charging with AC Outlet 120W Peak Power Bank(Solar Panel Optional) for Home Backup Outdoor Emergency RV Van Hunting
description:
EFFICIENT CHARGING: Use the adapter included in the package to charge the power station from 0 to 80% in 2hrs, and use the this power supply to charge other devices at the same time while charging, which could be equivalent to a charging strip , improving the efficiency of uses. It can be charged efficiently with MARBERO's dedicated 30W or 60W solar panel, suitable for outdoor or backyard camping
COMPACT & PORTABLE DESIGN: Super slim size(6.5 x 4.6 x 3.1 inches), the portable power station is about the size of a basic DSLR; With the strong yet lightweight(3.2lbs) plastic case, plus an ergonomic handle, it's quite suitable for outdoor camping, road trips, backyard camping, home, school, office,etc.
MULTI-OUTPUT: There are 8 output ports could be used at the same time, and the built-in BMS system ensures stability and safety. The car DC charger output cable(included) will supply power to car refrigerators, vacuum cleaners, and air compressor in emergencies. USB and USB C PD ports are compatible for all the mobile phones(1hr full), tablets(1.5hrs full), laptops(2hrs full), it's an essential unit for road trips. The two AC output ports will power home appliances, such as small TVs, game consoles, projectors, inflatable beds, drones, etc.
BRIGHT LED LIGHTS: With three levels of brightness, the portable power bank can be used as a large-capacity professional flashlight when needed, which is very suitable for use when the car breaks down, power outage at home, or a tent power supply. The 1st level can be used for 68hrs(light up a small room), the 2nd level can be used for 48hrs(lighting for walking at night), and the 3rd level can be used for 37hrs(light up the living room). In case of emergency, there is SOS flashing mode, use the international SOS frequency to call for help.
SECURED & ASSURED: The Battery Management System (BMS) allows for voltage control, temperature control and more advanced safety operations. Designed with the cooling vents on the back to help this portable power supply keep the temperature constant, its internal electrical elements will not be damaged by high temperatures. When it comes to short circuit or overload, it automatically shuts off the power to protect itself and the powered devices. By using the cylindrical batteries which meets UL safety standards. UL drop tested (3 drops from 0.9m on any kind of surface).
PACKAGE INCLUDES: 1*MARBERO Portable Power Station M82,1*Power Adapter,1*Cigarette Lighter Output,1*User Manual

top_reviews.review_body: I bought this device almost a full year ago when our power went out and it worked excellent for us in need of emergency! It came early next morning after ordering it the night before. It had full power, and we were able to charge our devices and small lights till our power came back on. It charges our devices fast and still has plenty of power to charge more devices. It lasts long! And when you charge the device itself, it's incredibly fast too. I love that it has plenty of ports, has an LED flashlight, an LED battery indicator, a handle and is high in quality. This power station is so worth the cost. EXCELLENT device! I'm very happy with it!

top_reviews.review_headline: Reliable Portable Power Station for Camping & Emergencies
top_reviews.review_body: This portable power station is a must-have for outdoor trips and home backups. It charges my phone, laptop, and camping lights all at once with no issues. The battery holds a charge for weeks when not in use, and it recharges quickly via solar panels or wall outlet. The LED display is easy to read, showing remaining power and charging status clearly. It’s lightweight and compact, fitting right into my backpack. Solid build quality—definitely worth the investment.

We live in a 10 so we use us to charge our phones every single night. Only thing is is that if we plug a fan into it it only lasts an hour but anything else like smaller electronics it lasts a very long time. Super durable charges up very fast battery life stays long our top quality product. The brightness on it is great. There’s three different levels that you can use. You can use it for a light and you can also use it to charge your electronics as well.

UPDATED: Company ultimately issued me a replacement unit which solved the problems I was experiencing. Still bummed my first unit had problems but appreciate getting quality service, and conceptually love this product and the idea of having a portable laptop / cell phone battery bank.ORIGINAL: I'm a professional sound recordist and bought this (along with a solar panel by same company) to be able to recharge laptop, phone, and other small electronics while in the field. On paper it's great, I like the compact design, the flashlight is a nice feature, and it has a ton of ports. The problem is that my model seems to suffer from unpredictable, sudden battery charge loss. E.g. sometimes it behaves as expected (I've done a ton of recharge for the watt-hours of my devices and this battery, so understand what I'm asking of it) - other times the battery will either self-discharge and go from 4 bars to zero, or start recharging a cell phone and then totally fail. As a result I don't consider it trustworthy to bring into the field and unfortunately that's kind of a deal-breaker for a device like this. I'll be contacting the manufacturer to see if they'll offer a replacement battery, but I'm not sure I'll be able to trust it again which is a shame. If you're a professional and have low tolerance for this device failing, I'd purchase something else. But if it's a fun toy for recreational use, this will probably work for you.

I’m constantly on the road for work and it’s complicated finding somewhere to charge your laptop or phones. This portable power pack works great and powered my laptop and phone when I am running low on battery. I charged this overnight and it typically lasts me a couple of days. Yes I get a couple of charge off of it and I like how small it is. If it’s perfect in my backpack with my laptop.

I got this in August 2024 and it is only now January 2025 and it doesn't seem to be working correctly. I just tried to use it to power my laptop while it itself is plugged in and it was fine for a little while but all of a sudden stopped doing anything and won't power the laptop any longer. I have the Marbero battery plugged in and I pressed the Power button and then pressed the Outlet button. Like I said, it was fine, the blue battery bars were full and the red plug picture lit up. Now the blue battery bars is only showing one, it's blinking, and when I try and turn on the Outlet button it blinks red quickly but doesn't do anything. I've checked the outlet this device is plugged into and it's working fine and other things that are plugged into the same outlet are working no problem.I originally gave this a great review and I thought it was great and very helpful but it hasn't even been a year and I've only used it I think two other times (aside from when I first got it to test it out) so I'm really annoyed by this. Below is my previous review.I work from home so that means that I need to have my modem/router and laptop plugged in and getting juice for the entire time I’m working. Thankfully, my shifts are only two hours at a time and neither my router nor laptop are all that power-hungry. But any time that I’m not able to work that’s time that I’m not getting paid for and that’s obviously a problem. My power has gone out more than once while I’ve been working so I’ve been looking for something like this for a while and it happened again recently so I decided enough was enough. I got this hoping that I can prevent losing any work time (and money.)I’ve looked at other, similar power stations, but they were either way too expensive, way too big/overkill for my needs, or both. This one was inexpensive and had a coupon and it seems to be able to do what I need. I especially like that it has regular outlets for things that have two or three prongs (like my modem/router). In addition to that it’s got multiple USB ports, a USB C port (for my laptop), and even has a port where you can plug in a car charger (it also includes the car charger adapter thing). In addition to that, there is a big flashlight that is nice and bright and has three brightness levels and an SOS mode. If you do use the flashlight keep in mind that aside from that eating up power from the device, the brightness that you have it set to will determine how long you’re able to use it. In other words, if you have it at the brightest level you won’t be able to use it as long as if you have it at the dimmest level. The same thing goes for what you’re charging with it. If you’re using it to charge a phone you will get more use out of it than if you’re using it to power a computer. Different devices use different amounts of power so it’s hard to say how much of a charge you will get from what you’re using it for.The entire device is quite small, smaller than I realized when purchasing it, and is also very light. I think it would be useful to have when road-tripping, camping, backpacking, etc. because of how tiny and portable it is.I also want to point out that you can get a compatible solar panel to use with this which can help charge it and recharging it via a wall outlet will bring you up to around 80% in about two hours. Now, there are others out there that will charge to around 80% in less time, but they’re also quite a bit more money. This one also has these safety features that I wanted to point out: Short-circuit Protection, Low-voltage Protection, Over-current Protection, Over-voltage Protection, Over-load Protection. So in simple terms, you don’t need to worry about it lol.

top_reviews.review_headline: The Best Portable Charger for Every Need!
top_reviews.review_body: I’ve tried many power banks before, but INIU has quickly become my go-to brand. Their 20,000 mAh, 65 W power bank is the most powerful, compact, and reliable charger I’ve ever owned.Charging Ability: With 65 W output, it enables me to charge almost all my devices at their maximum charging speed. For my Samsung S22 Ultra, it takes about an hour to charge back to full. I mainly start charging my phone again throughout the day when it's around 30% and to get up to around 80% again with this portable charger takes about 30 minutes and only takes away around 15 to 20% of the portable charger's full capacity. I also bought this charger to help charge my Rog Ally, but I haven't tested it yet. I estimate it could charge the Rog Ally to full again pretty quickly, but use up around 70% of its power. I can confidently say though I can get multiple phone charges no problem without worrying this will run out of battery with 20,000 mAh.Size: I love the size of this mini portable charger. Even with 20,000 mAh, it’s compact enough to meet airline carry-on requirements and fits neatly into my backpack or travel pouch without feeling like dead weight. It is around half the size of my first Inui charger that I've bought, which is the normal size. I feel like this one is small enough to also slip into your pocket, no issue.Design: The build feels premium and durable. I like the easy-to-use on-screen display, which works by pressing the power button once. A big thing I like about this one in particular is the cord that is tied to the side of the device. This cord makes it so convenient to charge my phone when I don't have any wires with me on hand. The cord on the side of the device is USB-C and supports up to 100 watts.If you need one reliable charger for a phone, laptop, or travel emergencies, INIU is where it’s at. I’ve used cheaper brands, but nothing comes close to this perfect balance of speed, capacity, and portability. It's absolutely the best power bank I’ve ever owned.

Assuming this works as well as a similar product, I'm excited! Fast charging of the bank and quick charging of my iphone, I pad, and even my laptop. This device may be shorter than some others, but it is a bit thicker. Its heavy, but worth the space in my purse. The more compact version fits well in a small purse (abo it the size of a couple compacts) . This unit is convenient because it comes with a small charging cable and built in storage (although i wouldn't trust using the cable as a carry strap) Assume all is good if I don't update the review!

I already owned an INIU portable charger, and because it worked so well, I decided to purchase another one. Just like the first, this one charges my devices quickly and reliably. It’s compact, easy to carry, and holds a great charge. Very happy with the quality and performance—definitely a brand I trust for portable chargers

top_reviews.review_headline: Awesome power in a little package! Definitely worth the value. A++
top_reviews.review_body: I love the simplistic design. I got the yellow one which looks really bright and forth coming with my electronics setup. The included USB c cable is smart.. since it connects as a carry strap. The mini is a perfect size and it functions well to keep a charge on my laptop. A word of advice for someone needing to charge such a device.... The 65w charger doesn't really add to the charge of my HP 4k screen laptop while I'm using it. The fans kick in to keep the motherboard and battery cool while charging thru the dedicated USB which eats up the charge while charging. There's no way around it tho!! However it does maintain its charge for an hour or more with the battery connected.

Cons: 65 watt output is highly questionable. My laptop charger is 65watt and charges it fast while using. This charger using the correct port rated for 65watta barely charges during use. It does keep it at where it is. If it’s at 80% charge it will stay at 80% for the entire time. If I had to guess the actual output I would say 35watts. It is just enough to hold the same charge percentage the laptop was at when plugged in for 4-5hours. I have tried 240 watt rated USB C cables it does seem to charge a hair faster and with this will slowly charge. It is still no where near as fast as the 65watt wall charger that comes with the laptop.Pros: even though it does seem to have enough output to charge it more while using it is just enough to not lose any laptop battery and continue to use the laptop for almost 5 hours! Which is extremely impressive my laptop alone maybe gets 2.5 hours. So this adds about 4.5-5hrs to that giving up to 7 hours of constant use which is awesome.To sum it up this charger with is capacity was almost had the price of Anker and I green. It works great for the price . I am confident Anker and Ugreen do charge at the advertised 65watt. I am confident this one does not do that. It is still fast enough to work fine with you laptop I suggest plugging it in when your laptop is at 80% or higher. It will hold that level for the entire time you use it.

top_reviews.review_headline: Awesome charger, perfect weight and size with a lot of power
top_reviews.review_body: I really like this power bank it is well designed and the perfect size to power up for several days. It has a crisp display that tells the battery percentage digitally. I got the yellow one and I really like the color. I like how the battery charges up fast and charges at 45 W. If you’re a fan of Iniu you should add this to your collection and also get the 25,000 mah battery as well. The attached cable is also such a great idea. Which I prefer a longer cable so I just carry around a couple of 2 foot charging cables and it works out just fine.

top_reviews.review_headline: Works well, holds a good charge
top_reviews.review_body: Charges a lot of things before it runs out, takes a while to recharge but nothing abnormal. It does seem to charge as a fast charger, so I wouldn't use it every time because that's not good for your battery. It's got some weight to it, but it will fit in your purse if it's at least a medium sized purse. Don't leave it in your car, I feel like that's something everybody should know. Just don't ever leave these things in your car.

I like that it has a braided cord for durability and two USB-C options for input and output. It supports 45W superfast charging, compatible with my Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra. I initially thought it didn't, but it turns out that you have to plug the phone in while the charger is off for superfast charging to kick in. If you click the power button first before plugging in the phone, you'll get slow charging. It too 22% of the battery to charge my phone from 13% to 80% in 35 minutes. Great value for cost.

I gave this power pack a very strenuous workout on a recent trip. It recharged two phones enough to keep them actively gaming all day and into the evening. The phones I used are iphone 16 pro max and iphone 13 pro max. The small size of the power bank made it easy to keep in my pocket. The length of the built in usb-c is pretty short so I used a separate cable to make it easier. The power bank also charged pretty fast and was easily ready for the next day of adventures. It didn't have any issue charging two phones at the same time and did not heat up. All in all, I am really pleased with this power bank and feel it's performance justifies its cost.

top_reviews.review_headline: Compact, Portable, No Wires, Gets Hot!
top_reviews.review_body: This was my first Magsafe battery. I had some older Qi wireless chargers and was familiar with heating issues. But these were older and lower power. Without the Magsafe feature, they were finicky and fussy and didn't properly fit around camera lens protrusions. I barely used these devices.This Anker 621 has a lot of promise. The Magsafe feature locks tight and the dimensions do not interfere with the camera protrusions, even with a case on the phone. It's compact and slim, and most importantly, does not require wires. It can be easily carried in a backpack or pocket without an accessory bag for wires and cables.When I first unpacked the battery, I fully charged with with a USB-C PD charger. It charged at 1A/5V. I used it to inductively charge my iPhone 14 Pro intermittently over the next few days. After about 10% charge, I determined the heat buildup to be intolerable. Therefore, I only used the pack intermittently. That worked fine for me over several days. When the battery was empty, it took 20Wh to fully charge. The battery was advertised for 5000mAh, which is impossible to measure without disassembling the unit. However, we can calculate 20Wh / 5V / 0.9 = 4400mAh (where 0.9 is the efficiency of the voltage converter for USB). This is below advertised capacity, but there’s a lot of assumptions in the conversion.Then I used a USB-C cable to charge my iPad Mini. It transferred 14.8Wh at about 9 Watts before exhausting. The round-trip efficiency was high, because recharging took 14.8Wh to fully recharge. I suspect I didn’t get the full 20Wh capacity with the iPad because the higher charge demand choked the battery before it could get fully empty.Using the inductive charge mode, both devices get hot relatively quickly. The switching power electronics get particularly hot. I can easily feel where the transistor is located beneath the plastic shell. The inductive transmitter loop gets warm on the battery and the receiver loop gets warm on the phone. Lithium-ion batteries degrade proportionally to temperature (the Arrhenius relationship), accelerating +25% degradation rate for every 10C, this is bad for the phone.In summary,• The battery delivers 20Wh of energy. If using inductive (Qi and Magsafe), much of the energy is converted to heat, so perhaps only 10-15Wh is actually delivered to the battery.• If using high-current devices using a charge cable, such as an iPad, the high current chokes the battery a bit early, stopping at 15Wh.• My back-of-the-envelope calculation shows 4400mAh vs. rated 5000mAh. I suspect the battery is not delivering rated capacity. Anker should be rating these batteries in Joules (watt-hours) instead of Coulombs (ampere-hours).• Inductive charging is inefficient, losing a quarter to half of energy to heat. This is not Anker’s fault but a result of real-world physics. With my iPhone 14 Pro, I saw about 10C rise per 10% of battery charge. I never let it progress beyond this point.• Lithium-ion batteries accelerate aging degradation due to the Arrhenius relationship about +25% per 10C temperature rise. Therefore, prolonged use of inductive charging will ultimately age your battery prematurely.• This battery is very worthwhile because I don’t have to carry an accessory kit with wires to use. Just slap it on the back of the phone and go. Plus, if frees the connector to use with wired headsets when using.

:UPDATE 3/11/2026 - Anker absolutely honored their commitment to their product and issued me a replacement. They were quick to respond and to mitigate the issue I had with their product. Thank you ANKER!!I’m a huge Anker fan and a loyal consumer of their products, so this is the first time I’ve encountered an issue with one of their products. The charging itself works fine, the problem is that the magnetic charger gets very hot, which disrupts charging until the temperature drops. I have another brand with the same specs that doesn’t have this issue.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Really good product.Delivered without damage and the build quality of Anker is great. Not too bulky! It gets heated a bit when overused, but the magnets are very strong!It always says slow charger while using this! Not even optimal speeds is concerning, but it saves the phone from shutting down.

top_reviews.review_headline: Superior Power Density & Wide Compatibility—Finally Solved My "Picky" Laptop Issue
top_reviews.review_body: Overall Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐After testing several portable solutions, the Anker Prime 20,000mAh (220W) has become a staple in my mobile kit. I’ve been putting this through its paces on-the-go, and it has consistently met high expectations, particularly regarding power delivery (PD) negotiation.Technical Highlights:• Reliable PD Negotiation: My wife’s work laptop is notoriously "picky" regarding its handshake with third-party chargers. After cycling through multiple power banks that failed to provide a consistent charge, this Anker Prime unit handled it flawlessly. It delivers the sustained wattage required for high-draw devices without throttling.• Total Output Efficiency: With a 220W max output, I can effectively fast-charge multiple devices—including a MacBook and an iPhone—simultaneously. It eliminates the "low power" warnings I’ve experienced with lesser 65W or 100W banks.• Monitoring & UI: The on-device digital display is excellent for real-time telemetry (input/output wattage and estimated recharge time). Additionally, the Bluetooth app integration is surprisingly useful for monitoring the bank’s health and managing charging cycles from a distance.• Form Factor: Despite the 20,000mAh capacity, it remains TSA-compliant and relatively compact for its power class.The Verdict:While the price point is higher than entry-level 20k mAh banks, you are paying for the high-wattage circuitry and the intelligent power distribution. If you have sensitive or high-demand electronics that reject standard chargers, the performance here justifies the investment.Pros: High-output ceiling, excellent build quality, intuitive app controls.Cons: Premium price (though the performance delivers the ROI).

Purchased this Anker Prime bank based off some travel blog suggestions. I've used Anker before with excellent results. I've been wanting a larger bank for long travel days so I grabbed Anker's larger bank.Overall feel in my hand is quality. Slightly heavy but you need the cells to have this amount of capacity. Keep in mind the tradeoff with capacity versus weight. This isn't something you want to carry in your pocket, even if it'll fit. But on a plane charging your device from a personal Item, it's perfect.I don't have the Anker Prime base but this charged pretty quick out of the box with a USB charger. I didn't measure the time but I went to check on it and it was at 100%. If you're worried a out charge speed I'd recommend the Prime Charger base.From 100% on this bank, I was able to charge my Z Fold 7 from 37% to 80% and the power bank only dropped to 86%. I was able to charge my phone and wife's phone several times (not from extremely low power) before needing to recharge this power bank. The value is questionable but Anker is known for quality and so far it's living up to that expectation.Works well with both my Samsung phones and every cable I've used on it. My main charging cable is a UGreen with built in charging monitor.I wasn't to sure about using an app for a power bank but it was easy to setup and use. This power bank is connected via Bluetooth and I can see it's current charge. It has all the charger parameters for the USB A and USB C ports to monitor in real time via Bluetooth.

top_reviews.review_headline: Perfect for long days when on the go
top_reviews.review_body: Slim and compact which is perfect for carrying if you’ll be out on a day trip. This is much better than having to carry a larger and heavier power bank. It doesn’t get very warm when I’m charging my iPhone 15PM via MagSafe. If I charge my iPhone by USB cord as a typical power bank but not attached by MagSafe, it does get warmer but it will also charge faster. You can leave this attached to your iPhone by MagSafe but charge using a USB cord if you hold on to the button for a few seconds. Charging this way does leave the battery almost coffee cup hot. It seems the battery gets hotter with wired charging and when attached by MagSafe, the extra warmth from the iPhone itself makes the battery hotter like a cup of coffee for comparison. Not dinging Anker for this since all power banks get warm to hot when charging.Note that all power banks can’t charge a device at the power banks’ maximum capacity since there’s some energy loss (about a third of the total capacity) when charging a device. This is noted in a few of the product images but not really detailed in the description. The compact size, portability, and MagSafe/Qi more than makes up for this since I really only need this for long days where I might not have access to a power outlet or on the go without having enough time to plug into an outlet. I don’t need to recharge my phone 100% and the Anker Nano Power Bank provides more than enough to get me through a long day.The white case has a matte finish. The sides appear to be aluminum. I avoided the black one since photos from other user reviews show a lot of finger prints and smudges. Build quality seems really solid.Magnets seem pretty strong; stronger than the MagSafe puck sold by Apple. It can rotate with enough force when pushed from the bottom corner but there is an alignment magnet. Otherwise, it attaches pretty securely with MagSafe. It’s as secure or more secure as any MagSafe accessory made by Apple.Only minor caveat is that it can be easy to accidentally press the power button if the power back is attached by MagSafe and you’re holding and using the device. This will disable MagSafe charging. If it stops charging and you know there’s power left, just press the button again.

This is a really good product. I use it with MagSafe, and it sticks very well. It has a solid grip on the phone and is very slim, so it doesn’t bother. It can fully charge an iPhone and has lights that show how much battery is left. I got the black color, just be careful, it scratches easily and the silver underneath shows.

top_reviews.review_headline: Perfect Portable Charger for My iPhone 17 Pro Max
top_reviews.review_body: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️☆I fell in love with the color the moment I opened the box—it looks even better in person. I brought it with me on a trip to San Francisco, and its portable size and lightweight feel made it easy to carry in my pocket and bag. Definitely 5 stars for size and weight.I tested the power on my iPhone 17 Pro Max, starting at 18%, and it charged up to 80% in one session. It did get slightly warm while charging, but nothing extreme compared to other Qi magnetic power banks I’ve owned. top_reviews.review_headline: Slim, fast, and convenient, but a bit too slippery
top_reviews.review_body: This is a very good product overall. It’s slim, lightweight, and extremely easy to carry, making it ideal for everyday use. The Qi2 charging works fast, and the power bank itself also recharges quickly, taking about an hour and a half, which is very convenient.The design is clean and modern, and it looks great when attached to the phone. Performance-wise, it does exactly what it promises.That said, the surface material is very smooth. When holding the phone while charging, it can feel slippery, and I’ve dropped it a few times because of that. A slightly more textured or less slippery finish would make it more comfortable and safer to use.Overall, I’m very satisfied with the performance and portability, but the grip could be improved.

top_reviews.review_headline: Great Little Power Bank for my Starlink Mini
top_reviews.review_body: Unlike the Anker power bank, this Ugreen 25000 mAh power bank seems to handle pass-through charging correctly which is impressive for such a small power bank. I use this with a 60 watt solar panel for my Starlink mini setup and have had no issues so far. One issue that arises with some other small power banks supplying power to a Starlink mini and receiving power from a solar panel is the fluctuations in wattage from the solar panel to the power bank, and similar fluctuations in wattage between the Starlink and the power bank due to Starlink differing power requirements during operation, and differing solar conditions during charging. This unit seems to work similar to a UPS. The Anker power bank (which seems to be very popular) has a reputation for dropping out during passthrough charging, thus causing the Starlink to reboot and never maintain a solid connection. In my testing, the Ugreen power bank appears to have no issues maintaining proper wattage between both connections during these fluctuations. My only real issue with the Ugreen power bank is that I wish the display were brighter as it's difficult to see in bright sunlight, however, you can still see the charge percentage well enough most of the time but just not the other display information.I've seen a few comments that complained about the charge rate for this power bank but I'm more than satisfied with the 65 watts (still fast) rate of charging even compared to the 140 watts of charging claimed for the Anker. The thing is, overly aggressive charge rates will only result in fewer cycles for the overall battery life, and with a small power bank such as these, the charging time isn't all that long anyway for either one if charged at the full rate. Actually, I normally only charge my Ugreen at about 25 watts as I don't want to reduce the number of cycles I'll have available in the long run, and the capacity lasts a long time for charging cell phones, etc. while away. In addition, my power bank is primarily used for my Starlink setup and will stay completely charged while on solar if I want it to. Most power banks throttle the charge rate when nearing capacity anyway so I don't think the charge rate if this unit is an issue as it's still very fast at 65 watts. Remember, you'll need a higher than normal wattage usb-c charger to charge at the maximum rate anyway for either the Ugreen or the Anker. A slightly lower charge rate than max is no issue for me and greatly extends the life of my power bank. Even Bluetti recommends that you not use their "Turbo Charge" mode often as it degrades battery lifeWith my current setup, in full sun, the solar panel will charge the power bank at about 20 to 30 watts while still keeping power to the Starlink with no dropouts, but you need to make sure you have the correct cables and that they can handle the wattage required for this setup. Starlink recommends a minimum 100W cable for the Starlink and I simply use the cable that came with the power bank for charging from my solar panel. My Starlink to power bank cable is a usb-c to barrel connector cable, the AGVEE 6.6ft Starlink Mini USB C to DC Cable rated for 140 watts. Also I use the FlexSolar 60 watt portable solar panel for charging off grid. In my testing I've found the fully charged Ugreen 25000 mAh power bank will power my Starlink Mini for between 3.5 to 4 hours with no solar input. I tested the capacity while running movies on a laptop while connected to the Starlink and had no power dropouts. If connected to the 60 watt solar panel the capacity is infinite in decent sun as the Starlink Mini consumes less than 20 watts most of the time while in use.I've also tried this setup with various Bluetti power banks with the following results:-Bluetti Elite 10, This power bank will not run the Starlink from usb-c (solar input or not) but will if using the 120v power supply and cable that came with the Starlink.-Bluetti EB3A, No issues.-Bluetti Elite 100, No issues.I've included some photos of my Starlink setup equipment which I store in a Harbor Freight Apache 3800 case. Note that, if you turn the Starlink mini facing up in the case, you can still use it from inside the closed case as the case surfaces and padding seem to cause no apparent degradation of the signal. The battery seems to maintain a cool temperature during this but the Starlink may start to overheat with the case closed after a while. This type of setup would be good if it were raining but you still wanted to power your Starlink mini off grid. The Starlink can handle the rain but I doubt the power bank could.Hope this is helpful.

top_reviews.review_headline: Power bank that JUST WORKS, as it should

Can charge my 4500mAh phone 4 times, my iPad Pro 1.5 times, and charges a 5,000mAh phone 3 times (about).Does have super fast charging, and fast recharge w/ 60W Ugreen charger I have in about 40 mins. Like the display info it gives. Shows watts, amps, and simple osciliscopd type line. Also does headphone trickle charge mode, which can use for phone if charging overnight or phone doesn’t have a battery setting to stop charging when complete. Comes with usb charging cable, velvet pouch w/ draw string so can store in there (can also buy a hard Eva shell case for this as well, it I did not).Highly recommend, as very handy for when power goes out, day trips, or to have in bag when need charging and don’t have wall plug. Can also charge other devices as well, 3 devices at a time all very power draws, but mileage will very on those devices battery charge level as depends on power bank “battery” and device battery level “want it at”.So, if want a hefty metal power bank, that can take 3 devices of various size, earbuds/headphones/controlers/etc….and charge them k owing will work, this for you. Power bank HAS AUTO SHUTOFF so if leave on, will turn off, and will turn off after awhile if say phone at 100% and done…power bank auto shuts of for me which nice so not draining battery all night. Also like truly super fast charging as well, as not all power banks have this feature even though says.Used to charge cellphone and earbuds weekly if wondering.My score 4.5/5 B/c screen could have blue in color, not green, which would have been easier to see, imo

top_reviews.review_body: Love this charger. I have a couple of older electronics (phone and iPad) that aren’t holding a charge as well as the should anymore. top_reviews.review_headline: Portable charger for a reasonable price
top_reviews.review_body: So far I really like this portable charger. It’s about the same size as other portable chargers I have purchased. So I’m not sure about their claim for smallest. I like that it has the cord attached so it’s easier to carry or pull out of a bag.It got to full charge pretty quick once I plugged it in after it arrived, which was also quick. It charges my phone quite a bit faster than most other portable chargers of similar wattage and much faster than magnetic portable chargers. I like that it has usb-c compatibility. It holds a charge for a good amount of time and can charge my phone a few times before it needs to be plugged in for a charge. Once drained it takes less than 4 hours to get to 100% again.It’s nice that the battery percentage is shown on the screen. That way you can tell if you’ll need to charge it or not before using it for your needs.It feels quality and smooth. The color I got is black which is fine. It’s easy to use. It can get a bit warm depending on what you are charging and where. Be careful using it outside in the heat or in the sun. It’s never gotten too hot, but I could see how it might happen.Overall for the price, I would recommend it for anyone looking for a decent portable charger of this size for charging small devices like cell phones. If something changes over time I will come back and update my review.

top_reviews.review_headline: Great Power Bank That Gets the Job Done
top_reviews.review_body: I’ve been really happy with this power bank so far. It holds a charge for days when not in use and can fully charge my S24 Ultra around three times on one full charge. It doesn’t heat up at all, which is great. It does take a while to recharge itself, and it’s a bit on the heavier side but honestly, for the price, it’s a solid deal. Nothing fancy, just reliable and gets the job done.

I am truly grateful for the customer support at INIU — I have purchased three of these power banks in total (one of this very item and two of its older models which are no longer available). They are the perfect size and weight, even considering the bigger the capacity the bigger the size and greater the weight. Battery life has been great. Two USB-C and one USB-A is the perfect trio for my needs, the power bank and attached USB-C cable feel sturdy and well built, and this bad boy charges up my devices in exceptional speeds. Side note: I reached out to INIU for my defective power banks, provided video and photos for my claims, and they sent out my replacements in no time. Ten out of five stars, they have definitely gained a happy and returning customer.

This is a very good power bank. I bought this to recharge a tablet that I use at work and it does a great job. There is power to spare for recharging my IPhone. It is heavy, and takes a few hours to recharge via usb, but the build quality is good and I recommend it for a budget friendly power bank.
